info.title: Immersive - API
info.description:
### Introduction Our API allows you to integrate your existing systems and tools with our platform. Once authenticated, you can access your organisation's data by making REST API requests to available [endpoints](#description-end). This API is being actively worked on. Please [reach out](mailto:support@immersivelabs.com) with any specific use-cases for enhancement of its functionalities. **Note:** In all examples below, replace `{api_url}` with your region-specific API endpoint (e.g., https://api.us.immersivelabs.com, https://api.immersivelabs.online). ### Authentication You can generate an API key from the API Settings page in the platform. You will be provided an Access key and a Secret token which should be used in the call below: ```bash curl -X POST -d 'username={your_access_key}&password={your_secret_token}' {api_url}/v1/public/tokens ``` This will generate a token which will expire after 30 minutes.
Example token: ```json { "username": "{your_access_key}", "organisationId": 1, "accessToken": "{token_uuid}", "expiresAt": "2021-06-19T15:19:10.000+00:00", "expiresIn": 604799 } ```
You can optionally specify a scope value in the request body containing one or more scopes separated by space to limit the access level of the token. If no scope value is specified, the token will have full access available to the API key. Currently supported scopes are: - `account:read` - provides access to user account data, available to all API keys by default - `account:delete` - provides access to user account deletion, only available upon request - `account:write` - provides access to user account creation and updates - `assignment:read` - provides access to user assignment data, available to all API keys by default - `catalog:read` - provides access to available catalog content data, available to all API keys by default - `team:read` - provides access to team and team membership data, available to all API keys by default - `meta:read` - provides access to API meta endpoints, available to all API keys by default ```bash curl -X POST -d 'username={your_access_key}&password={your_secret_token}&scope=account:read assignment:read' {api_url}/v1/public/tokens ``` ### Requests Once you have a token, you will be able to make REST API requests about your organisation. An example endpoint might be `/v2/activities`. Here's an example of how you can use the curl command for this GET request: ```bash curl -X GET {api_url}/v2/activities -H "Authorization: Bearer {token}" ``` Please replace `{token}` with your access token.
Example JSON response: ```json { "page": [ { "id": "1b7dab3a-08c8-456f-8daa-4ee25c388372", "createdAt": "2020-08-25T09:35:38Z", "title": "Cyber Lab Example", "type": "lab", } ], "meta": { "hasNextPage": true, "hasPreviousPage": false, "nextPageToken": "...", "previousPageToken": null } } ```
### Pagination Paginated endpoints will always return the page data in an array with the key `page`. The top-level `meta` object contains information about the current pagination state. It shows if there is a next page, a previous page, and contains the tokens for navigation to each. To move to the next or previous page, reissue your request and include a page token in the `page_token` URL parameter. While paginating, you should not change the requested page size as it could result in missing data. ### Useful links - [Terms of service](https://www.immersivelabs.com/company/legal/terms-of-use) - [Contact Support](mailto:support@immersivelabs.com)

parameters.name: created_date_start
parameters.description: Filter accounts created on or after this date/time (ISO 8601 format).

parameters.name: created_date_end
parameters.description: Filter accounts created on or before this date/time (ISO 8601 format).

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Filter accounts by email address.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Filter by a specific account UUID

parameters.name: account_status
parameters.description: Filter by a specific account status

parameters.name: last_activity_date_start
parameters.description: Filter accounts with last activity on or after this date/time (ISO 8601 format).

parameters.name: last_activity_date_end
parameters.description: Filter accounts with last activity on or before this date/time (ISO 8601 format).

parameters.name: send_email_notification
parameters.description:
Whether to send a confirmation email to the account. Defaults to false.
                              Ignored if SSO is enforced for the organisation.

firstName.description: First name of the account holder

lastName.description: Last name of the account holder

timeZone.description:
Time zone for the account in IANA Time Zone database format
            (e.g., Europe/London, America/New_York)

post.description: Token scope required: `assignment:write`. This endpoint assigns accounts to an exercise asynchronously, the progress can be polled with the URL in the response's `Location` header.

location.description: Path to the endpoint for polling the asynchronous task's progress.
Path to the endpoint for polling the asynchronous task's progress.

post.description: Token scope required: `assignment:write`. This endpoint unassigns accounts from assignments asynchronously, the progress can be polled with the URL in the response's `Location` header.

location.description: Path to the endpoint for polling the asynchronous task's progress.
Path to the endpoint for polling the asynchronous task's progress.

409.description: Conflict. Returned when an asynchronous task is already in progress or when the exercise cannot be updated due to another conflict (for example, the exercise is closed or overdue).

location.description: Path to the endpoint for polling the asynchronous task's progress. Only present when the conflict is due to an asynchronous task already being in progress.
Path to the endpoint for polling the asynchronous task's progress.

409.description: Conflict. Returned when an asynchronous task is already in progress or when the exercise cannot be closed (for example, the exercise is pending or already closed).

location.description: Path to the endpoint for polling the asynchronous task's progress.
